title: Continuous-Time and Discrete-Time Systems
summary: The page discusses noncausal systems and their approximate physical realization through time delay, using an analogy of a prophet versus a wise man. It introduces a drill problem demonstrating a noncausal system and its 5-second delay realization. The page then transitions to defining continuous-time and discrete-time signals and systems, noting that discrete-time signals can arise from sampling continuous-time signals.
